suggested_fixes: Add TORCH_CHECK(d >= 3584 && d <= 4608, "d must be in [3584, 4608]") in check_inputs.
Add device consistency checks, e.g. TORCH_CHECK(W.device() == X.device() && W.device() == A.device() && W.device() == B.device(), "all inputs must be on the same CUDA device").
Optionally document that contiguous copies are intentional for correctness with non-contiguous inputs.
warnings: Does not explicitly enforce runtime d in [3584, 4608]; it will accept any d so long as tensor shapes are otherwise compatible.
Does not explicitly check that all tensors are on the same CUDA device; cross-device matmul will fail at runtime via ATen rather than in input validation.
Does not explicitly check contiguity before use, though this is mitigated by making contiguous copies internally.

Relies entirely on ATen matmul kernels rather than custom CUDA kernels, so explicit grid coverage, stream handling, launch checking, and alignment checks are delegated to PyTorch internals rather than being visible in this source.
